channel.title: City of Belvedere News
channel.description: Official news and updates from the City of Belvedere, California

item.title: City Council Report: Mallard Pointe, Budget and Staffing
item.description: At its June 8 meeting, the City Council received an update on the Mallard Pointe Project and approved a nine-month extension of its tentative subdivision map, now running through March 19, 2027. This extension provides additional time for the developer to complete the remaining steps required for approval of the final map. In the meantime,...

item.title: A New Volunteer, Reappointments, and One Open Seat
item.description: The city is pleased to announce the reappointment of Ghazaleh Jamei and the appointment of Jacob Linetsky to the Planning Commission, the reappointment of Tony Hooker to the Belvedere-Tiburon Library Agency Board, and the reappointment of Bob McCaskill and Patrick Feder to the Finance Committee. The city extends its sincere thanks to these dedicated volunteers...

item.title: Concerts in the Park Sets the Stage on Father’s Day
item.description: Bring the family to Belvedere Community Park on Sunday, June 21 at 4pm to dance and sing along with Super Diamond, an energetic group celebrating the life and legacy of Neil Diamond. This is the 29th year of free summer concerts for the Belvedere community. Come join the fun! The concert series is sponsored by individual...

item.title: Celebrate Juneteenth in Tiburon on June 21
item.description: Before or after enjoying Concerts in the Park in Belvedere, head to Main Street in Tiburon for a local celebration of Juneteenth—a holiday that honors freedom and promotes community engagement by celebrating Black history, culture, and resilience. This free community event takes place Sunday, June 21, from 3–7pm and will feature live jazz and hip hop performances,...

item.title: Humane Rat Control Pilot Program
item.description: In response to resident concerns about increased rat activity, the city is beginning a six-month mitigation pilot program. As part of the pilot, the city will install secured bait stations using Evolve Rat Birth Control in targeted areas. This non-lethal fertility-control bait is designed to reduce rat reproduction over time, offering a humane and safer...

item.title: City Council Election: November 3, 2026
item.description: On November 3, 2026, the city will hold a general municipal election to fill two seats on the City Council. To become a candidate, you must be over 18 years of age and a registered voter in Belvedere. The nomination period begins July 13, 2026, and ends on August 7, 2026. If one of the...

item.title: Be Aware of Coyotes Protecting Their Young
item.description: Spring through summer is coyote pup season. Coyotes create temporary dens just for bearing and caring for their pups. Like all parents, coyotes vigorously protect their young from perceived threats—especially dogs. Be sure to walk your dog on a leash no longer than 6 feet, and always be aware of your surroundings. A coyote that...

item.title: Registration for Run Tiburon 5K / 10K Now Open
item.description: Registration is now open for the August 30 Run Tiburon! With its views of Mt. Tam, the Golden Gate Bridge, Angel Island, and San Francisco Bay, this idyllic fall race is unlike any other. Funds from Run Tiburon support the Ranch and its programs. This event is sponsored by the Tiburon Peninsula Foundation. Register Now!

item.title: One-way Traffic Control on Tiburon Blvd. & Stewart Dr. Starts May 28
item.description: This week, Caltrans will begin retaining wall work on Tiburon Boulevard at Stewart Drive to stabilize the roadway and upgrade the guardrails. Work begins Thursday, May 28 and is expected to be completed by the end of August. During the project, one-way traffic control will be in place Monday to Friday between 8pm and 7am....

item.title: Mallard Pointe Project Coordinator Meet & Greet: June 4
item.description: In April, Belvedere contracted with Eric Seabrook, President of Phillips Seabrook Associates, to serve as project coordinator providing oversight of the Mallard Pointe Project on behalf of the city. We invite you to meet Eric and his team on Thursday, June 4 at 5:30pm, at the Belvedere Community Center, 450 San Rafael Avenue. Attendees will...

item.title: City Council Report
item.description: At the May 11 City Council meeting, Mill Valley Refuse announced a 2.2% rate increase to cover rising operational costs, starting with the next billing. The Council also adopted a new policy governing the responsible use of AI by City staff. Additionally, staff presented a $14.48 million preliminary budget for fiscal year 2026/2027, prioritizing fiscal...

item.title: Welcome New Admin Technician: Jules Fogarty
item.description: After a thorough public recruitment process, City Hall is pleased to welcome Jules Fogarty as our new Administrative Technician. Before joining the city, Jules worked in the nonprofit sector, focusing on securing and managing funding for food bank networks. Outside of work, you can catch him playing ultimate frisbee around the Bay. He coached UC Berkeley's...

item.title: Free Curbside Vegetation Pick Up Begins Tuesday May 19
item.description: To help you rid your property of fire-prone vegetation and provide more defensible space around your home, Belvedere is offering its Annual Curbside Vegetation Pickup Program on Tuesday mornings from May 19 through June 30. Place cut vegetation in a neat pile at the road edge in front of your property before 8am on one...

item.title: Plan Ahead: Emergency Preparedness Course May 20
item.description: The Tiburon Peninsula faces many potential hazards including: earthquakes, wildfires, storms, flooding and landslides. On May 20 at 1pm, participate in a two-hour disaster preparedness course hosted by Laurie Nilsen, the Emergency Operations Center Coordinator for the Tiburon Peninsula. This important training opportunity will be held in the Belvedere Tiburon Library Founders Room. Learn practical...

item.title: Final Tiburon Traffic Study to Be Presented May 20
item.description: Curious about what’s next for traffic and safety in your community? The Tiburon Transportation and Infrastructure Study Final Report will be presented to Tiburon's City Council on May 20, 6:30pm at Tiburon Town Hall. The report recommends ways to reduce congestion, improve safety, and boost mobility on the peninsula. To learn more — join the...

item.title: Mark Your Calendars: Yellow Bus Passes on Sale June 1
item.description: The Tiburon Peninsula Traffic Alliance (TPTA) is excited to announce that yellow bus passes for the 2026–27 school year will go on sale beginning June 1 at 9am at www.tiburontraffic.org. Don't wait! Passes are sold on a first-come, first-served basis, and Belvedere routes sell out quickly. We encourage families to purchase theirs as soon as sales open. Email info@tiburontraffic.org with any...

item.title: Volunteer Opportunites for City Committees
item.description: The Belvedere City Council will be interviewing candidates at their June 8 meeting to fill the following vacancies: Planning Commission: 2 seats, one 2-year term, one 4-year term. Finance Committee: 2 seats, 4-year terms. Belvedere Tiburon Library Agency: 1 seat, 4-year term. To be considered for one of these volunteer positions, apply by Friday, May 29...

item.title: California Primary Election: Tuesday, June 2
item.description: By now, your June 2, 2026 Statewide Direct Primary Election ballot has made it to your mailbox. Once its ready, you can either put it in your mailbox or use the official drop box in front of the Belvedere Community Center, 450 San Rafael Ave. Need more information? You can check your voter registration, track your ballot,...

item.title: Belvedere Film Fest Kickoff Party: June 12
item.description: Attention aspiring filmmakers! If you’re thinking of entering a film in the Third Annual Belvedere Home Made Film Festival, don’t miss the Kick-off Party on Friday, June 12, at 5pm at the Belvedere-Tiburon Library. Learn how to participate in the film festival and sign-up for free filmmaking workshops. The workshops - offered from June to...

item.title: City Council Report
item.description: At its April 13 meeting, the City Council approved a contract with Phillip Seabrook Associates to provide construction oversight for the Mallard Pointe project. Their work will include organizing project submittals, tracking approval conditions, ensuring compliance, and serving as a liaison between the City, residents, the applicant, contractors, and engineers. The firm brings expertise in inspections, engineering...
